articles.title: How Gnutella Scaled to Handle Query Traffic
articles.summary: Gnutella scaled by transitioning from a simple flood routing system to a more selective and efficient Query Routing Protocol, QRP, which allowed peers to advertise compact summaries of available files behind them. This change improved scalability and reduced bandwidth usage, making it possible for the network to handle millions of concurrent queries without a global file index or coordination servers.

articles.title: Jacobian Conjecture from Wolfram MathWorld
articles.summary: The Jacobian conjecture asserts that a polynomial map having a nonzero constant Jacobian determinant is an automorphism in the plane. This condition is easily shown to be necessary but remains open for higher dimensions. A counterexample was found by Alpoge in July 2026, which he credits to the AI system Fable. The conjecture is one of Smale's problems and has been studied extensively in algebraic geometry and differential equations.

articles.title: Human mathematicians are being outcounterexampled
articles.summary: Human mathematicians are being outcounterexampled as their work is being formally verified and validated by AI tools, including those developed by companies like Logical Intelligence. This process involves using interactive theorem provers to check the accuracy of mathematical statements, which can be time-consuming and require significant expertise. The formalization of complex mathematical concepts, such as class field theory, has been a long-standing goal for mathematicians, but it remains challenging due to its complexity and lack of short proofs.

articles.title: Agent swarms and the new model economics

The focus is on understanding how these swarms work well enough to engineer them deliberately, with experiments showing promising results in tasks such as building SQLite from scratch in Rust. This approach also enables internal applications like finding vulnerabilities, raising test coverage, and generating synthetic training data.

articles.title: How we measured AI writing across arXiv, and where the measurement breaks
articles.summary: We measured AI writing across arXiv by scoring the full text of 12,750 papers and found that about a third of new ones read as machine-written. Our detector, calibrated for academic writing, flags 20% of genuine pre-LLM scientific text and recovers 85% of AI academic text at a 0.4% false-positive rate. The results show that the measurement breaks down over time, with significant increases in flagged share within months of ChatGPT's release.

articles.summary: InvisiCaps: The Fil-C Capability Model Fil-C ensures memory safety of all operations in the C and C++ language by dynamically tracking what object a pointer should be allowed to access using a capability system for pointers, which prohibits accesses out of bounds or to freed objects. This model also aims to preserve fanatical compatibility with widespread idioms that don't lead to exploitation unless they violate the rules, while providing reasonable performance and memory usage.
